detail: Price moving against thesis (-1.3% since open). Proceed with caution or wait for reversal.
agent_brief: Momentum (100) and trend_persistence (85) are both elite, and with VIX at 17.5 (normal regime, all multipliers at 1.0x baseline), these signals carry full weight. The three-model ensemble should show strong agreement: the momentum-only model loves this (momentum 100 + trend_persistence 85), and the linear model should as well. However, three concerns temper enthusiasm: (1) analyst downgrades flagged as a catalyst (Δ=-2), which creates headwind tension against the momentum thesis; (2) price is moving against the call thesis (-1.3% since open per mechanical check), and the $105 strike already requires a 3.4% breakeven move — a high bar for a weekly hold; (3) confidence at 38% is barely above the 25% hard gate and well below the 50-70% sweet spot where calibrated confidence peaks (0.58). The delta at 0.51 is above our 0.35 target, meaning we're paying for more premium than our methodology wants. Proceed, but this is a tight-leash trade — if price doesn't reclaim $107+ by Tuesday, charm decay on this slightly ITM call will erode quickly.

detail: Delta shifted from 0.52 to 0.86 (price moved from $22.30 to $22.25). Consider re-selecting strike closer to current price.

detail:
[AGENT OVERRIDE] The strongest pick of the three. Trend_persistence (95) and momentum (90) are both near-ceiling, and all three ensemble models should agree directionally — momentum-model loves the momentum/trend combo, and the linear model benefits from strike_efficiency (75) rounding out the score. The 1.59% gap UP confirms the call thesis (below the 2% gap-against-skip threshold, and it's gap *with* thesis). Breakeven at $230.10 is only a 1.0% move from entry — the most achievable of the three picks. Delta at 0.51 is above our 0.35 target (paying more premium), but with price action confirming direction, this is acceptable. Key risk: MS is a financial sector name in a tight-credit, narrow-leadership environment — our backtest shows tight credit yields only 50.4% accuracy. The COT extreme short positioning (13.7 percentile) provides a contrarian bullish tailwind (+2.5 for calls per methodology), which partially offsets the credit headwind.
Original mechanical signal: GO — Early action confirms call thesis (+0.5% since open).
